fields.name: fixed random seed#If this is non-zero, the effect will usually be exactly repeatable
skip fraction#chance that this event will be skipped entirely
delay bounds:seconds#delay before this event takes place
delay bounds:seconds#delay before this event takes place
camera mode#Currently, the only supported use is to turn parts off in first-person view.
velocity bounds:world units per second#initial velocity along the location's forward, for decals the distance at which decal is created (defaults to 0.5)
velocity bounds:world units per second#initial velocity along the location's forward, for decals the distance at which decal is created (defaults to 0.5)
velocity cone angle:degrees#initial velocity will be inside the cone defined by this angle.
MARKER NAMES
fields.definition:
In addition to the marker in the render model there are several special marker names:

replace
Replace allows you to use the same effect with different markers. Damage transition effects support this for example.

gravity, up
The direction of gravity (down) and the opposite direction (up).  Always supplied

normal
Vector pointing directly away from the surface you collided with. Supplied for effects from collision.

forward
The 'negative incident' vector i.e. the direction the object is moving in. Most commonly used to generated decals. Supplied for effects from collision.

backward
The 'incident' vector i.e. the opposite of the direction the object is moving in. Supplied for effects from collision.

reflection
The way the effect would reflect off the surface it hit. Supplied for effects from collision.

root
The object root (pivot). These can used for all effects which are associated with an object.

impact
The location of a havok impact.

water_surface
Point of the effect projected to the water surface above it. Otherwise it just returns the effect position.

structure_surface
Use this for effects that might be going off underneath soft surface that you want to be on the surface

child
The location of the last detonated projectile, in case the parent object is a weapon (beams only)

fields.name: sort bias#use values between -10 and 10 to move closer and farther from camera (positive is closer)
percent velocity to inherit#flag must be checked above
near fade range:world units#distance beyond cutoff over which particles fade
near fade cutoff:world units#distance in front of camera where fade is complete*
near fade override:world units#distance in front of camera where fade is complete
LOD feather out delta#0 defaults to 5.0, minimum is 0.0001
dynamic resolution multiplier#lower numbers cause particle systems to render more at low-res - must check flag above
bounding radius estimate*:world units#used if override is zero
bounding radius override:world units#used if non-zero
axis scale#NOTE - setting this will break automatic bounding sphere calculation, you must enter radius manually
translational offset:world units#XYZ controls that offset the emitter's origin from the original location
relative direction#XYZ vector that changes the initial rotation of the emitter
emission radius:world units#defines the size of the emitter
emission angle:degrees#determines the angle at which particles are emitted
emission axis angle:degrees#determines the max tilt for particle axis
particle starting count#number of particles that are spawned at the birth of the effect
particle max count:0=unlimited#max number of particles allowed to exist at one time
particle emission rate:particles per second#number of particles that are spawned every second from the emitters
particle lifespan:seconds#the number of seconds a particle will live after emission
particle self-acceleration:world units per second per second
particle initial velocity{particle velocity}:world units per second
particle initial rotation rate{particle angular velocity}:360 degree rotations per second
particle tint:RGB#controls the overall tint of the particle
distribution exponent#exp==.5 even distribution, exp>.5== tighter

options: do not re-use parts when looping#This option will hurt performance on effects with very short duration.
use parent position but world orientation
cannot be restarted#Will help performance for parallel events of different lengths, if the effect is unlikely to be reused
force use own lightprobe#Do not adopt parent object's lightprobe, even when it's available
particles die when effect ends{particles die when event ends}
track sub-frame movements#When effect moves multiple times within one frame, update location each time
create even when event loops back to self
particles continue as usual when offscreen{skip visibility test}{cinematics}
particles die when effect ends{die when orphaned}
use synchronized random seed#synchronized across particle systems
use world orientation#particle system uses local-space position but up is always 'global up'
render in spawn order#first particle spawned renders first (at the back), last particle spawned renders last (front)
dynamic particle resolution#use distance and multiplier (below) to tune high- or low-res rendering
volume emitter particle velocities are random#By default, particles emit radially away from the emitter center.  This option gives them random velocity instead.  This only applies to volume emitters.
particle emission time{emitter time}{particle emit time}
